source: Things to do in Raleigh this Weekend! — Thingstodo919

events.name: Got to Be NC Festival
events.tagline: A free, family-friendly celebration of North Carolina food, farming, and live entertainment.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 12–8 PM · Sat 10 AM–8 PM · Sun 10 AM–6 PM
events.freeNote: Free admission; some activities and marketplace items may cost money
events.description: The Got to Be NC Festival is a three-day event at the North Carolina State Fairgrounds featuring local food and drink vendors, live music, carnival rides, farm animals, tractors, and agricultural exhibits. The festival celebrates North Carolina’s agricultural heritage and is rain or shine.

events.name: Third Friday Durham Art Walk & Gallery Crawl
events.tagline: Downtown Durham comes alive with free art receptions, exhibits, and performances.
events.address: Durham City Center, Foster Street and Chapel Hill Street
events.description: Third Friday Durham is a monthly downtown art walk and gallery crawl featuring free receptions at galleries, museums, and creative spaces across Durham. Most venues host programming from 6:00 to 9:00 PM.

events.name: Adult Nights: Social in the Shire
events.tagline: A 21+ after-hours Middle-earth takeover of the museum with science, music, games, and Tolkien fun.
events.venue: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
events.description: Adult Nights: Social in the Shire is a 21+ after-hours event at the North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences in Raleigh. The evening features interactive science stations, themed activities, music, games, local entertainment, and food and drinks available for purchase.

events.name: NCMA Movies at Moore Square: Poupelle of Chimney Town
events.tagline: Free family movie night under the stars in downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Moore Square and the North Carolina Museum of Art present a free outdoor screening of Poupelle of Chimney Town as part of the NCMA Movies at Moore Square series. The event includes themed programming and food trucks before the film starts at 7:15 p.m.

events.name: Bike Movie Double Feature
events.tagline: Pedal to the Rialto for a double feature of cycling cinema.
events.description: Oaks and Spokes presents a double feature of bike-themed movies at the Rialto Theatre, featuring Breaking Away and another cycling classic. Arrive early for doors at 6:15 PM.

events.name: Club 90s
events.tagline: A high-energy nostalgia dance party spinning mid-2000s anthems all night.
events.description: Club 90s is a DJ-led dance party centered on the music and style of the mid-2000s, with a nonstop mix of club and pop favorites. It’s scheduled for Friday night at The Ritz in Raleigh and is an 18+ event.

events.name: Comedy Show: Grant Lyon and Matt White
events.tagline: A night of stand-up comedy featuring Grant Lyon and Matt White at The Cary Theater.
events.description: Grant Lyon and Matt White bring stand-up comedy to The Cary Theater for one night only, with an approximate runtime of two hours.

events.name: Candlelight: Featuring Vivaldi's Four Seasons and More
events.tagline: A candlelit chamber concert reimagining Vivaldi and more at a historic Wake Forest venue.
events.description: Candlelight presents a 60-minute performance of Vivaldi’s Four Seasons and related selections at The Historic Wakefield Barn. The concert is part of the Candlelight series, featuring live classical music in a candlelit setting.

events.name: Goodies: 2000s Hip Hop Nite
events.tagline: A night of 2000s hip hop with DJ Uniq spinning the biggest jams of the era.
events.description: Goodies returns to Durham for a night of 2000s hip hop featuring DJ Uniq spinning the biggest jams from artists like Dr. Dre, Beyoncé, OutKast, and more. This 21+ event runs from 9 PM to midnight at Motorco Music Hall.

events.name: Cary Night Market
events.tagline: A lively Friday night market with local artisans, food trucks, drinks, and entertainment in Downtown Cary Park.
events.description: Cary Night Market is a monthly evening market at Downtown Cary Park featuring 45 to 50 local vendors, including artisans, prepared food vendors, and food trucks. The event also includes music and entertainment and is open to the public at no charge.

events.name: Carolina Ballet presents Beauty and the Beast
events.tagline: A live-music ballet reimagining of the classic fairy tale.
events.venue: Memorial Auditorium at Martin Marietta Center for the Performing Arts
events.scheduleNote: Fri 7:30 PM · Sat 2:00 PM & 7:30 PM · Sun 2:00 PM
events.description: Carolina Ballet presents Beauty and the Beast, a world-premiere full-length ballet by Artistic Director Zalman Raffael with live music. The production runs May 14-17, 2026 at Raleigh Memorial Auditorium in the Martin Marietta Center.

events.name: Gianmarco Soresi
events.tagline: New York–based stand-up comedian brings sharp observational humor to Goodnights Comedy Club.
events.description: Gianmarco Soresi performs stand-up comedy at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh, showcasing his sharp societal observations and energetic stage presence.

events.name: Matt Ross
events.tagline: Austin comic Matt Ross brings sharp, laid-back stand-up to Goodnights Comedy Club.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 7:30 PM · Sat 7:30 PM · Sun 7:30 PM
events.description: Matt Ross is an Austin-based comedian, writer, and actor performing a weekend run at Room 861 at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh. The show is scheduled across Friday through Sunday with evening performances.

events.name: Matilda the Musical
events.tagline: Roald Dahl’s hit musical brings high-energy songs and a fearless young heroine to Raleigh.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 10 AM & 7 PM · Sat 2 PM & 7 PM · Sun 2 PM
events.description: Roald Dahl’s Matilda The Musical is being presented by Theatre Raleigh in North Raleigh. The production follows a brilliant girl who uses imagination and courage to stand up for what’s right.

events.name: Champagne & Friends
events.tagline: Explore global sparkling wines with expert tastings and culinary pairings.
events.description: An educational 90-minute tasting event celebrating global sparkling wines beyond Champagne, featuring expert-led tastings of Prosecco, Cava, Cremant, and international selections. Chef Doreen Colondres demonstrates professional pairing techniques and cooking with sparkling wine, accompanied by curated hors d'oeuvres.

events.name: L'École Annual Fundraising Gala for Education
events.tagline: An elegant evening celebrating education and community at the Governor's Mansion.
events.description: L'École French International School of Raleigh hosts its Annual Fundraising Gala to support academic excellence and launch the 2026–2027 Classroom Grant Fund. The evening features community networking, live and silent auctions, and brings together international families and professionals from across the Triangle.

events.name: 2026 NASCAR Regional Season - Capital City 170
events.tagline: Professional NASCAR regional racing featuring Late Model Stocks, Mini Stocks, Legends, Bandoleros, and Bombers.
events.scheduleNote: Gates 6 PM · Qualifying 7:15 PM · Racing 8:30 PM
events.description: The Capital City 170 is a NASCAR regional racing event featuring multiple racing classes competing on Wake County Speedway's 1/4 mile asphalt track. This Military Night event honors U.S. veterans and active personnel with high-speed short track racing action.

events.name: Bull City Metal Fest
events.tagline: Two days of heavy local and regional metal at Stanczyks.
events.freeNote: Day passes $20; weekend pass $30 (plus fees on ticketing site)
events.description: Bull City Metal Fest returns to Stanczyks with two days of local and regional bands. The lineup runs Friday evening and Saturday from early afternoon into late night, with day and weekend passes available.

events.name: An Evening with Noah Guthrie
events.tagline: An intimate night of Americana, folk, and soul with Noah Guthrie.
events.description: Noah Guthrie performs at The Wake Forest Listening Room in Wake Forest on Friday evening. The event is listed as all ages with doors at 6:45 PM and showtime at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Brodie Cormack Band, Josh Rhyne and Silvertones w/ Kyle Mullins & Sophie Taylor
events.tagline: A Friday night lineup of country-rock and Americana at Lincoln Theatre.
events.description: Brodie Cormack Band, Josh Rhyne and Silvertones perform with Kyle Mullins and Sophie Taylor at Lincoln Theatre in Raleigh on Friday, May 15, 2026. Doors open at 7 PM and the show starts at 8 PM.

events.name: Burns & the Torchlights x Rafael Green x Island Moons
events.tagline: An evening of indie and jazz-grunge sounds at Cannonball Music Hall.
events.description: Burns & the Torchlights join Rafael Green and Island Moons for a live music show at Cannonball Music Hall in Raleigh, featuring jazz-grunge and indie sounds.

events.name: Groovin' in Garner Welcomes Rockzilla
events.tagline: Free downtown concert night with classic-rock energy and local food trucks.
events.description: Groovin' in Garner Welcomes Rockzilla is a free outdoor concert in downtown Garner featuring the Raleigh-based cover band Rockzilla. The event takes place on the lawn at Garner Recreation Center and includes food trucks plus beer and wine available for purchase.

events.name: Heluva Dance Social - Friday Night Country
events.tagline: Beginner-friendly country swing dancing with live instruction and social dancing.
events.description: Join Heluva Dance for a beginner-friendly country swing social featuring a lesson from 8–9 PM followed by social dancing until 11 PM. No partner needed—rotations ensure everyone participates in this welcoming downtown Cary event.

events.name: Overcome Fest IV - Day 1 w/ Bitter End
events.tagline: A heavy-hitting hardcore kickoff with Bitter End and friends at KINGS.
events.description: Day 1 of Overcome Fest IV takes over KINGS in downtown Raleigh with Bitter End plus additional hardcore acts including Eyez Wide Shut, One by One, Distrust, and Quick to Judge. Doors open at 6:00 PM and the show starts at 7:00 PM.

events.name: Resurrection: A Goth Night
events.tagline: DJ SKYLAN spins goth, darkwave, and post-punk classics in the shadows.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: A goth night featuring DJ SKYLAN spinning darkwave and post-punk classics from artists like The Cure, Depeche Mode, Joy Division, and more. 21+ event at The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop.

events.name: You Me & Kyle
events.tagline: Indie rock at Slim’s with a late-night downtown Raleigh set.
events.description: You Me & Kyle is scheduled to perform at Slim's Dive Bar in downtown Raleigh on Friday, May 15, 2026. The event is listed for 8–11 PM and appears to be a ticketed live music show.

events.name: Suite Surrender Spring Play
events.tagline: A wartime hotel farce full of mistaken identities and diva-sized egos.
events.description: Set in 1942, this comedy follows two Hollywood divas whose feud explodes when they’re accidentally assigned the same suite at the Palm Beach Royale Hotel. The play features mistaken identities, overblown egos, and classic farce-style humor.

events.name: Phenom G3 Live
events.tagline: Premier grassroots basketball showcase for ages 9U–17U.
events.description: Phenom Hoops brings its premier grassroots basketball showcase to Raleigh, featuring travel ball tournaments for boys and girls ages 9U–17U with a 4-game guarantee and NCAA certification.

events.name: Bull City Cocktails Tour
events.tagline: Sip, stroll, and taste your way through Durham’s cocktail scene on a guided downtown walk.
events.description: Bull City Cocktails is a small-group guided walking tour through downtown Durham with stops at three cocktail spots. Each stop includes a full craft cocktail and food pairing, along with local history and stories about the Bull City.

events.name: The Spring Ephemeral: Alysa Cantor
events.tagline: An evening art opening exploring transience through cyanotypes and mixed media.
events.description: Liberty Arts presents a solo exhibition by artist Alysa Cantor in Durham Food Hall’s Looseleaf Gallery. The show features experimental cyanotypes and mixed media inspired by seasonal change and fleeting natural cycles.

events.name: We Set Out Terms - A Black Erotic Praxis: Power, Consent, Liberation Opening Reception
events.tagline: An opening reception for a group exhibition exploring Black erotic expression, embodiment, and liberation.
events.description: This opening reception launches an exhibition featuring 25+ selected artists whose work engages themes of embodiment, power, consent, and liberation. It takes place at Zeitgeist Gallery & Bar in Durham.

events.name: Secret Society Improv Show
events.tagline: A fast, funny night of improv from Mettlesome’s Secret Society.
events.description: Mettlesome’s Secret Society performs an improv comedy show with a live audience in Durham. The show is scheduled for Friday, May 15, 2026, from 7:00 to 8:00 PM.

events.name: Mint Field in Durham
events.tagline: Mexico City dream pop and shoegaze with a haunting live edge.
events.description: Mint Field, the Mexico City-based band led by Estrella del Sol and Sebastián Neyra, performs at The Pinhook with support from Big Mistake. The show is listed as all ages and features the group’s blend of dream pop, shoegaze, and trip-hop textures.

events.name: Durham Labor Choir Spring Concert
events.tagline: A spirited evening of labor songs, solidarity, and worker power.
events.description: Durham Labor Choir’s spring concert features labor classics and new re-writes centered on textiles and women of the labor movement. Partial proceeds benefit choir-chosen strike funds.

events.name: Barn Dance with The Fuzzy Catfish Blues Band
events.tagline: A soulful night of rhythm and roots at the farm with pay-what-you-can admission.
events.description: Join Sunrise Community Farm Center for an energetic barn dance featuring The Fuzzy Catfish Blues Band. This pay-what-you-can event celebrates local music and community with live blues, dancing, and a welcoming atmosphere for all ages.

events.name: VIETRI Spring Warehouse Sale
events.tagline: Score steep discounts on handcrafted Italian home goods during VIETRI’s biggest shopping event of the season.
events.scheduleNote: Fri 10 AM–5 PM · Sat 10 AM–5 PM · Sun 1–5 PM
events.description: VIETRI’s Spring Warehouse Sale features discounted handcrafted Italian dinnerware, flatware, glassware, garden accessories, and home décor, with inventory refreshed each day. Shoppers can browse in person at the Vietri Outlet in Hillsborough or shop online during the sale window.

events.name: Elizabeth Cotten Freight Train Blues Concert Series
events.tagline: Free Friday night roots and blues music celebrating Elizabeth Cotten's legacy.
events.description: A five-week concert series featuring roots and blues musicians celebrating the legacy of Elizabeth Cotten and the Carolina Piedmont musical tradition. The opening night on May 15 features Brenda Evans, Gail Ceasar, and Kelley Breiding, with a special presentation of Elizabeth Cotten's historic Martin guitar.

events.name: Artsplosure - The Raleigh Arts Festival
events.tagline: Downtown Raleigh fills with juried art, live music, and family fun for one free weekend.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 11:00 AM–7:00 PM · Sun 10:00 AM–6:00 PM
events.description: Artsplosure is Raleigh’s long-running downtown arts festival featuring an art market with about 175 juried artists, live performances on two stages, and hands-on activities for kids. The 2026 event takes place May 16–17 on Fayetteville Street in downtown Raleigh.

events.name: North Carolina Courage vs. Chicago Stars FC
events.tagline: NWSL soccer action as the Courage host Chicago at home.
events.description: The North Carolina Courage host Chicago Stars FC in a regular-season NWSL women's soccer match at WakeMed Soccer Park in Cary.

events.name: Pickin' in the Park
events.tagline: A free Knightdale BBQ-and-bluegrass celebration with family activities and a charitable twist.
events.description: Pickin' in the Park is a new annual community festival at Knightdale Station Park featuring North Carolina barbecue, live bluegrass and Americana music, and a kids' corner with activities. A portion of proceeds supports Raleigh Rescue Mission’s The Garden.

events.name: Wheels on Academy
events.tagline: Downtown Cary’s free car show rolls in with classic cars, live music, food, and Touch-a-Truck fun.
events.description: Wheels on Academy is Cary’s annual downtown car show on North Academy Street featuring classic and custom vehicles, an EV showcase, and a Touch-a-Truck area for kids. The free event also includes live music and local food vendors.

events.name: Durham Greek Festival
events.tagline: A free weekend of Greek food, music, dancing, and church tours in Durham.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 11 AM–8 PM · Sun 11 AM–7 PM
events.description: The Durham Greek Festival features authentic Greek food and desserts, live Greek music, folk dance performances, and church tours at St. Barbara Greek Orthodox Church. The festival runs rain or shine and also includes a marketplace and taverna with Greek wine and beer.

events.name: Bimbé Celebration
events.tagline: A free, family-friendly celebration of African and African American culture, music, food, and art.
events.description: The Bimbé Cultural Arts Festival is Durham’s annual celebration of West African, African American, and local community culture. The event features live performances, arts and crafts, traditional food, and family activities at Rock Quarry Park.

events.name: Durham Ballet Theatre presents Aladdin
events.tagline: A classic tale of magic, romance, and adventure brought to the stage in ballet form.
events.description: Durham Ballet Theatre presents Aladdin at the Carolina Theatre of Durham with performances on Saturday afternoon and evening. The production draws from the original folktale and blends classical ballet with aerial dance and contemporary movement.

events.name: John Crist Live
events.tagline: Stand-up comedy with special guests Wellington Ojukwu and Maggie Depalo.
events.description: John Crist performs live comedy at DPAC with special guests Wellington Ojukwu and Maggie Depalo. Children under 6 are not permitted; all attendees require a ticket.

events.name: Ole 60: Smokestack Town Tour with special guest Rob Langdon & The Bends
events.tagline: A high-energy country stop bringing Ole 60’s Smokestack Town Tour to downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Ole 60 brings its Smokestack Town Tour to Red Hat Amphitheater in Raleigh with special guests Rob Langdon & The Bends. The concert is scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026, with doors opening at 6:00 PM and the show starting at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Godsmack with Stone Temple Pilots and Dorothy
events.tagline: A hard-rock triple bill brings a big summer concert to Walnut Creek.
events.venue: Coastal Credit Union Music Park at Walnut Creek
events.description: Godsmack headlines this Saturday night concert at Coastal Credit Union Music Park at Walnut Creek, with Stone Temple Pilots and Dorothy on the bill. The show is part of Godsmack’s 2026 Rise of Rock World Tour.

events.name: Toadies: The Charmer Tour
events.tagline: The Toadies bring their Charmer Tour to Raleigh for a Saturday night rock show.
events.description: Toadies perform at The Ritz in Raleigh on Saturday, May 16, 2026 as part of The Charmer Tour. The show is an all-ages general admission concert with support acts subject to change.

events.name: Pop Up Skate Park
events.tagline: Roll into Dix Park for a free all-ages skate day with ramps, rails, and good vibes.
events.description: Skate Raleigh is hosting a pop-up skate park at Gipson Play Plaza in Dix Park. The all-ages event includes real skate features and volunteers on hand to help beginners and experienced skaters alike.

events.name: ICON: Motown & Beyond with the North Carolina Symphony
events.tagline: A high-energy symphonic celebration of Motown and legendary pop hits.
events.venue: Meymandi Concert Hall at Martin Marietta Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: The North Carolina Symphony presents ICON: Motown & Beyond at Meymandi Concert Hall in downtown Raleigh. Guest vocalists Capathia Jenkins and Ryan Shaw join the orchestra for a program of hits by Michael Jackson, Prince, Whitney Houston, Diana Ross, Stevie Wonder, and others.

events.name: Cars and Coffee Raleigh: Exotic and Classic Car Show and Market
events.tagline: Classic and exotic cars, coffee, food, and a market kick off an early morning at Boxyard RTP.
events.description: This Cars and Coffee Raleigh event features an exotic and classic car show, on-site vendors, coffee, food, and drinks at Boxyard RTP. The event is family and pet friendly and runs for one morning only.

events.name: HBOT4Heroes' Ride 4 Their Lives with Raging Bull Harley-Davidson
events.tagline: A police-escorted charity motorcycle ride followed by a free community celebration.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 10 AM registration · Kickstands up at 12 PM · Post-ride party 1–4 PM
events.freeNote: Ride registration $25 per rider / $15 per passenger pre-registration; $30 / $20 day of
events.description: HBOT4Heroes' Ride 4 Their Lives is a 60-mile police-escorted charity motorcycle ride that starts and ends at Raging Bull Harley-Davidson in Durham. Riders return for a post-ride celebration with live music, food, vendors, and raffles; the party is free to attend.

events.name: Stuffed Animal Sleepover
events.tagline: Drop off your plush pal for an adventure-filled night at the Museum.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 4–5 PM drop-off · Sun 10 AM–12 PM pick-up
events.description: Drop off your stuffed animal for an overnight adventure at the Museum, then return the next day to reunite with your plush pal for a snack, activity, and photos from their night. Space is limited; each ticket includes one stuffed animal to stay overnight and one child and two adults for drop-off and pick-up events.

events.name: Preservation Durham 2026 Home Tour: Hope Valley Centennial
events.tagline: Tour eight historic Hope Valley homes in a centennial celebration of Durham neighborhood design.
events.description: Preservation Durham’s annual home tour celebrates the 100th anniversary of Hope Valley with access to eight homes in the neighborhood. The tour is hosted from Hope Valley Tennis Pavilion and runs Saturday and Sunday from noon to 4 p.m.

events.name: Crawfish Boil at Ponysaurus Brewing Co.
events.tagline: A big, messy crawfish feast in Ponysaurus’s Durham beer garden.
events.description: Ponysaurus Brewing Co. is hosting a traditional crawfish boil with fresh crawfish, andouille sausage, potatoes, and local corn. Pimm’s Cups and Hurricanes will also be available.

events.name: Amped for Stagville!
events.tagline: A donation-based benefit concert with live local music in support of Historic Stagville.
events.description: Amped for Stagville! is a donation-based benefit concert featuring Durham’s own Vertigo Conspiracy at Bull McCabe’s Irish Pub. Proceeds support Historic Stagville and its preservation and public history work.

events.name: Common Roots Festival for AAPI Heritage Month
events.tagline: Celebrate Asian Pacific American heritage with live performances, art, vendors, and food.
events.description: The second annual Common Roots Festival celebrates Asian Pacific American Heritage Month with live performances, local Asian artists, a vibrant vendor market, and delicious food. This free, all-ages event honors the diverse journeys and contributions of Asian and Pacific Islander communities.

events.name: Eurovision Song Contest Watch Party
events.tagline: Watch Europe’s biggest music competition live with fellow fans and a cold beer.
events.description: Glass Jug Downtown is hosting a free Eurovision Song Contest watch party with the international competition broadcast on the big screen. The event runs Saturday from 3:00 to 7:00 PM.

events.name: SINGamajig
events.tagline: An improvised musical for kids where audience suggestions become the story.
events.freeNote: $8 per audience member age 15 and under; $5 per adult, at the door
events.description: An improvised musical for all ages featuring a made-up story with songs inspired by audience drawings and suggestions. Help create the world with your participation.

events.name: Medieval Night
events.tagline: A free medieval-themed night of live music, mead, and playful tavern fun.
events.description: Medieval Night at Ment Beverage Co. features live music by Robert Joseph Finch, tavern snacks, mead tasting, and costume prizes. The event is scheduled for Saturday, May 16 from 7:00 to 10:00 PM and is free to attend.

events.name: Jambalaya Soul Slam Haiku Slam
events.tagline: A live, bracket-style haiku poetry showdown at Hayti Heritage Center.
events.description: Jambalaya Soul Slam presents an annual haiku slam at Hayti Heritage Center in Durham. Poets compete head-to-head with original haiku, senryu, or similar short-form poems, with judges selected from the audience deciding the winner.

events.name: 11:59 x Hobolectuals x Morior
events.tagline: A loud birthday show at Cannonball Music Hall with three high-energy bands.
events.description: This Cannonball Music Hall show features 11:59, Hobolectuals, and Morior in a birthday-themed lineup. Doors open at 7:30 PM, with music starting at 8:00 PM.

events.name: Battle Of The Bands - Final Four Championship
events.tagline: Local bands face off in a high-energy final-four showdown.
events.description: The Final Four Championship of Battle of the Bands brings together local bands for a live competition at The Portal HQ in Raleigh. The event is scheduled for Saturday evening and admission starts at $12.

events.name: Bleachers Listening Party
events.tagline: Hear Bleachers’ new album early at a free in-store listening party.
events.description: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop is hosting a Bleachers album listening party in its Record Shop space on Saturday afternoon. The event is listed as free and all ages, with no RSVP required.

events.name: Christian James // Khallori
events.tagline: A Saturday night indie rock double bill at Neptunes Parlour.
events.description: Christian James and Khallori are scheduled for a Saturday evening show at Neptunes Parlour in downtown Raleigh. Doors are listed for 7 PM, with the performance running from 7:00 to 9:45 PM.

events.name: Family Movie Night at Joyner Park
events.tagline: Watch Shrek under the stars with family-friendly pre-show activities.
events.scheduleNote: Sat 7:30–8:30 PM (pre-show activities 7:30 PM, movie 8:30 PM)
events.description: The Wake Forest Parks, Recreation & Cultural Resources Department presents Family Movie Nights at Joyner Park, featuring Shrek projected on a 26-foot inflatable screen. Pre-show activities for kids begin at 7:30 PM with the movie starting at 8:30 PM; food vendors will be on site.

events.name: Gouge Wrestling: Spring in the Ring
events.tagline: An outdoor pro wrestling showcase with high-energy local action at Rebus Works.
events.description: Gouge Wrestling presents Spring in the Ring, an outdoor professional wrestling event at Rebus Works. The showcase features live matches and family-friendly sports entertainment for all ages.

events.name: HAWT MESS: SLAYYYTER EDITION
events.tagline: A hyperpop rave takeover at Neptunes Parlour.
events.description: HAWT MESS: SLAYYYTER EDITION is scheduled at Neptunes Parlour in downtown Raleigh on Saturday night, with DJs from 10 PM to 2 AM. Listings indicate a $5 cover for the event.

events.name: Wolfpack Polka
events.tagline: A lively polka night with the Wolfpack at Slim's Downtown Raleigh.
events.description: Wolfpack Polka is scheduled at Slim's Dive Bar in downtown Raleigh on Saturday, May 16. The event features the Wolfpack Polka Band and is listed as free admission.

events.name: Lincoln X Woller
events.tagline: A late-night B2B DJ set at Neptunes Parlour.
events.description: Lincoln X Woller is scheduled as a B2B set at Neptunes Parlour in downtown Raleigh. The event is listed for Saturday night with a $5 cover and 21+ admission.

events.name: Music at the Market
events.tagline: Enjoy vinyl DJ sets and local food on the patio at House of Many Porches Market.
events.description: Join Oak City Soul Vinyl Collective DJs on the patio for live music and vinyl jams. House of Many Porches offers snacks, sandwiches, coffee, baked goods, wine, and beer for a perfect picnic experience.

events.name: Notes on View: Thomas Hinds (return performance)
events.tagline: Live music in the galleries brings Thomas Hinds’ return performance to NCMA.
events.description: Notes on View is a pop-up music series at the North Carolina Museum of Art featuring live performances inspired by works on display. This return performance by Thomas Hinds takes place in the galleries and is listed as a free event.

events.name: Oak City Voices present "Sing a Song of Shakespeare"
events.tagline: A choral tribute to Shakespeare’s wit, drama, and timeless words.
events.description: Oak City Voices presents a concert inspired by Shakespeare, featuring music by composers including Thomas Morley, Ralph Vaughan Williams, Amy Beach, and Sarah Quartel. The program sets text from seven plays and two sonnets to music.

events.name: On Pace House Dance Party
events.tagline: A deep, nonstop house-music club night built for serious dancers.
events.description: On Pace House Dance Party is a four-hour club event focused on minimal, deep, and hard-hitting house music. It is presented by Bitter Social at Bitter Social - Above Centro in downtown Raleigh.

events.name: Overcome Fest IV
events.tagline: A full-day hardcore and metalcore blowout at Lincoln Theatre.
events.description: Overcome Fest IV is a hardcore/metalcore music festival at Lincoln Theatre in downtown Raleigh. The lineup includes acts such as Terror, Pain of Truth, Rotting Out, Missing Link, Nasty, and Raleigh's own One By One.

events.name: Route 55 Jazz Orchestra
events.tagline: Big-band jazz gets the spotlight at Holly Springs Cultural Center.
events.description: Route 55 Jazz Orchestra is a community big band known for authentic jazz in the tradition of Basie, Kenton, and Miller. The group performs at Holly Springs Cultural Center for an evening concert on Saturday.

events.name: The Bleeding Hearts w/ The Temp Agency, Dead Rat Disco
events.tagline: A Saturday night of loud, local rock at The Pour House.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: The Bleeding Hearts play The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop on Saturday, May 16, 2026, with The Temp Agency and Dead Rat Disco. The show is scheduled for 8:00 PM with doors at 7:00 PM and is a 21+ event.

events.name: Write Now! 2026
events.tagline: A one-day writing conference for aspiring and established writers in the Triangle.
events.freeNote: Registration starts at $109; students/seniors $99; late $119; walk-in $125
events.description: Write Now! 2026 is a one-day, multi-track conference for fiction and nonfiction writers at the McKimmon Conference and Training Center in Raleigh. The program includes keynote speakers, breakout sessions, networking, lunch, and door prizes.

events.name: John-Robert at The Pinhook
events.tagline: Indie-folk singer-songwriter John-Robert brings a Saturday night set to Durham.
events.description: John-Robert performs at The Pinhook in Durham with support from nickname jos and Savanna Leigh. The show is listed as all ages, with doors at 6:30 PM and music beginning at 7:30 PM.

events.name: Monster Mayhem Drag Show
events.tagline: A freaky, creepy, fearsome drag night at The Pinhook.
events.description: Monster Mayhem Drag Show brings Stormie Daie / Rainy Daie Productions to The Pinhook for a late-night, 21+ drag performance in Durham. Doors open at 10:00 PM with the show starting at 10:30 PM.

events.name: Hillsborough Vintage Market
events.tagline: A monthly vintage and maker market at The Rookery in Hillsborough.
events.description: The Hillsborough Vintage Market is a recurring Saturday market featuring vintage finds and local vendors at The Rookery. The listing shows monthly dates throughout 2026, with hours from 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM.

events.name: Rocket League Tournament
events.tagline: Compete in a weekly Rocket League showdown at Ego Chall Esports.
events.description: A Rocket League tournament hosted by Ego Chall Esports in Chapel Hill. The event is listed for Saturday, May 16, 2026, with a $5 entry fee and a $50 Visa gift card prize.

events.name: RollFest 2026: The Wheel-y Wild West
events.tagline: Celebrate National Bike Month with bikes, skateboards, scooters, and more at this family-friendly community event.
events.description: Join a celebration of National Bike Month featuring bikes, skateboards, scooters, and more. The event includes skill-building activities, safety resources, and connections to local community programs.

events.name: Sensory-Friendly Puppet Playtime with Jeghetto
events.tagline: A welcoming puppet performance designed for kids and families who benefit from a calmer experience.
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Puppet Playtime with Jeghetto is a family-oriented program at Chapel Hill Public Library. The event is designed to be accessible and comfortable for children who do better with a lower-stimulation environment.

events.name: The Myers House | Halloween Replica Film House Visit
events.tagline: Tour the infamous Halloween replica house and hang out with fellow horror fans.
events.description: This guided exterior house visit at The Myers House NC includes a brief history of the replica, trick-or-treat candy, and a photo opportunity on the front porch. The event is held in Hillsborough and typically follows a limited, donation-based ticket format.

events.name: The DPAC Rising Star Awards
events.tagline: Central North Carolina’s top high school musical theatre talent takes the DPAC stage for one night only.
events.description: The DPAC Rising Star Awards is Central North Carolina’s high school musical theatre awards ceremony, featuring finalists and ensembles from participating schools across the region. Hosted by Clay Aiken, the event recognizes student performance and production achievements and selects finalists to advance to the Jimmy Awards in New York City.

events.name: Brier Creek Spring Food Truck Festival 2026
events.tagline: A free Sunday evening feast with 50+ food trucks, live music, and family fun.
events.description: The Brier Creek Spring Food Truck Festival returns on Sunday, May 17, 2026, at Brier Creek Corporate Center behind Brier Creek Commons. The event features more than 50 food trucks, live music, local vendors, and kids’ activities, with free admission.

events.name: Gettin' Piggy With It 2026
events.tagline: A barbecue-filled fundraiser with big flavors, local drinks, and live music for a great cause.
events.freeNote: General admission $150; Early Entry $200; children 11 and under free
events.description: The Frankie Lemmon Foundation's fourth annual Gettin' Piggy With It returns to Maywood Hall & Garden in Raleigh with nearly 30 pitmasters and chefs, barbecue, local beer and spirits, live music, and games. Proceeds benefit Frankie Lemmon School and Developmental Center.

events.name: Emo Philips
events.tagline: A surreal stand-up legend brings his deadpan, offbeat comedy to Raleigh.
events.description: Emo Philips performs a special stand-up show at Goodnights Comedy Club in Raleigh on Sunday, May 17, 2026 at 4:00 PM. Known for his distinctive voice and surreal observational humor, Philips is a long-running favorite in comedy clubs and on TV.

events.name: Triangle Youth Music Spring Orchestra 2026
events.tagline: Triangle Youth Music’s young musicians bring a full orchestra program to downtown Raleigh.
events.venue: Meymandi Concert Hall at Martin Marietta Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Triangle Youth Music presents its Spring Orchestra 2026 concert at Meymandi Concert Hall in Raleigh. The performance features youth orchestral ensembles and is scheduled for Sunday, May 17, 2026.

events.name: Live & Local!: Cary Town Band
events.tagline: Free live band music kicks off summer at Downtown Cary Park.
events.description: Cary Town Band performs as part of Downtown Cary Park’s Live & Local series, featuring local talent in a free outdoor concert setting. The event is held at the park’s Great Lawn Pavilion in downtown Cary.

events.name: NC Master Chorale: We Are Phoenix
events.tagline: North Carolina premiere of a moving choral work celebrating rebirth and resilience through poetry and music.
events.description: The North Carolina Master Chorale Youth Choir presents the North Carolina premiere of Timothy Takach's We Are Phoenix, a reflective choral work that uses Earth's cycles of rebirth as a metaphor for human resilience and transformation through poetry from multiple authors.

events.name: Rocket Music Booking 11th Anniversary Fundraiser & Celebration
events.tagline: A free anniversary party with live music, drinks, and fundraising at ZincHouse.
events.description: Rocket Music Booking marks its 11th anniversary with a fundraiser and celebration at ZincHouse Winery & Brewery in Durham. The event runs Sunday afternoon into the evening and is listed as free admission.

events.name: Arts at St. Luke’s presents May Musicale
events.tagline: A mixed bouquet of music featuring local musicians and special guests.
events.description: Arts at St. Luke’s presents the May Musicale, a concert featuring Kaye Saunders, David Arcus, and other St. Luke’s musicians performing a varied program of old and new music.

events.name: Cocktail Classes at Durham Distillery: Modern vs. Classic Cocktails
events.tagline: Learn how bartenders evolve classic cocktails with modern techniques and flavors.
events.description: This hands-on cocktail class places classic cocktails side by side with their modern counterparts, exploring how bartenders evolve flavor, technique, and presentation. Guests will learn foundational builds behind timeless drinks and discover how to riff on them with confidence.

events.name: Cocktails & Cuisine Class: Back to the Basics Edition
events.tagline: Learn bartending basics and enjoy craft cocktails paired with cuisine.
events.description: Join skilled bartenders for an interactive class where you'll learn to make three classic cocktails and enjoy them paired with cuisine. Gather your crew and sharpen your bartending skills on Sunday, May 17.

events.name: Verdi Requiem
events.tagline: A powerful performance of Verdi’s towering choral masterwork.
events.freeNote: $20 general admission; $5 non-Duke students; Duke students free
events.description: Presented by the Choral Society of Durham with Mallarmé Music, this concert features Verdi’s Requiem at Duke University’s Baldwin Auditorium. The performance is scheduled for Sunday, May 17, 2026 at 4:00 PM.

events.name: WISHING Women's Summit
events.tagline: A panel discussion spotlighting women’s paths and advice in sports.
events.description: The WISHING Women's Summit brings together professional women in sports to share their career paths and advice for the next generation. It is held in person at DICK'S House of Sport in Durham.

events.name: Yot Club at Motorco
events.tagline: Indie rock project Yot Club brings the SIMPLETON tour to Durham.
events.description: Yot Club, the project of Ryan Kaiser, performs at Motorco Music Hall in Durham on Sunday, May 17, 2026. The show is part of the SIMPLETON tour and features support from zzzahara.

events.name: Living History: From the Rock Wall (Rescheduled Date)
events.tagline: Hear local Black history brought to life through stories, poetry, and community memory.
events.description: In collaboration with the Marian Cheek Jackson Center, this event shares the history of Chapel Hill and Carrboro’s Black neighborhoods through local poets, speakers, and historians. Guests can also sample food from local Black-owned businesses carried at Weaver Street Market.

events.name: Steel String Brewery's 13th Anniversary!
events.tagline: Celebrate 13 years of Steel String with a festive taproom party in Carrboro.
events.description: Steel String Brewery is hosting its 13th anniversary celebration at the Carrboro taproom on Saturday afternoon and evening. The event runs from 2 to 9 PM and is listed as free to attend.

events.name: Not Another Music Festival
events.tagline: An intimate afternoon of live music, drinks, and food at Botanist & Barrel.
events.description: Botanist & Barrel hosts Not Another Music Festival in Cedar Grove with live performances, pizza, wine, beer, cider, and non-alcoholic drinks. The event is scheduled for Sunday, May 17 from 4:00 to 8:00 PM and has limited ticket capacity.

events.name: Bikes Mean Business: Self-Care Sunday
events.tagline: A community bike ride and self-care meetup for women, trans, and femme riders.
events.description: Bikes Mean Business: Self-Care Sunday is a Sunday cycling event hosted at The Bike Library in downtown Raleigh. It is listed as a self-care ride for women, trans, and femme participants.

events.name: Square One EP Release: w/ Larry., Times Tables
events.tagline: Punk rock band Square One celebrates their EP release with special guests.
events.venue: The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop
events.description: Square One, a punk rock band, celebrates their EP release with performances by Larry. and Times Tables at The Pour House Music Hall & Record Shop.

events.name: The Black Angels: Passover 2026 Tour w/ L.A. Witch
events.tagline: Austin psych-rock legends celebrate 20 years of their debut album with a full performance.
events.description: The Black Angels perform their landmark 2006 debut album Passover in full, celebrating its 20th anniversary, with support from L.A. Witch. The Austin-based psychedelic rock band will also play their greatest hits.

events.name: The Turkey Buzzards
events.tagline: A Sunday night rock set at Slim's Dive Bar in downtown Raleigh.
events.description: The Turkey Buzzards are scheduled to play at Slim's Dive Bar in downtown Raleigh on Sunday evening, May 17, 2026. Listings show the show at 7–9 PM at 227 S. Wilmington St.

events.name: Top Greene's "Adornment of Grace" Hat Show Contest
events.tagline: Celebrate Black culture and creative style at this hat show competition featuring vintage collections and ceramic art.
events.venue: John P. "Top" Greene African American Cultural Center
events.description: A celebration of Black culture and hat-wearing traditions featuring a best hat contest, vintage hat collections, ceramic sculptures by artist Erica Kendrick, and guest speaker Dr. Donna Corbett. All attendees are automatically entered to win a free 3-hour venue rental.
